things.name: Kumsusan Palace of the Sun
things.description: Mausoleum where Kim Il-sung and Kim Jong-il lie in state - the largest monument in North Korea

things.name: Pyongyang Metro
things.description: One of the world's deepest underground railways - decorated with murals, chandeliers and mosaics

things.name: Juche Tower
things.description: 170m granite obelisk symbolising Kim Il-sung's ideology - with views over the Taedong River

things.name: Kim Il-sung Square
things.description: Vast central square where mass military parades and mass gymnastics performances take place

things.name: Mansudae Grand Monument
things.description: 22m bronze statue of Kim Il-sung and Kim Jong-il - visitors are expected to bow and lay flowers

things.name: Arch of Triumph
things.description: Ten metres taller than the Arc de Triomphe in Paris - built for Kim Il-sung's 70th birthday

things.name: May Day Stadium
things.description: World's largest stadium with 114,000 capacity - home of the Arirang Mass Games spectacular

things.name: Mangyongdae Native House
things.description: Kim Il-sung's birthplace village preserved as a patriotic shrine

things.name: Koryo Museum (Kaesong)
things.description: 1361 Confucian college in the former Goryeo capital - UNESCO World Heritage celadon collection

things.name: Naengmyeon (Cold Noodles)
things.description: Pyongyang's most famous dish - thin buckwheat noodles in an icy broth with radish kimchi
